name: Nick Saban
blurb: Retired from coaching, not from being the most powerful man in Alabama.

name: Randy Owen
blurb: Frontman of the band literally named Alabama. Peak brand-state alignment.

name: Sarah Palin
blurb: Can reportedly see at least one polling place from her house.

name: Lisa Murkowski
blurb: Once won a Senate race as a write-in. Her vote has main-character energy.

name: Charles Barkley
blurb: Turrible jump shot. One (1) vote, same as you.

name: Alice Cooper
blurb: Shock rocker, golf fanatic, and one Phoenix-area ballot.

name: Larry Fitzgerald
blurb: Caught everything ever thrown at him. Your cancellation will be no exception.

name: Danica Patrick
blurb: Drives fast. Votes once. That's the rule.

name: Sarah Huckabee Sanders
blurb: Governs the state. Still only gets the one ballot.

name: Justin Moore
blurb: Sings about small towns. Votes in one of them.

name: Oprah Winfrey
blurb: She gives away cars, not ballots. She only has the one.

name: Kim Kardashian
blurb: Almost a lawyer. Definitely a voter.

name: LeBron James
blurb: Four rings, one vote. Democracy is the great equalizer.

name: Snoop Dogg
blurb: Drop it like it's a completed ballot in an official drop box.

name: Mark Zuckerberg
blurb: Knows everything about your vote. You can know about his (that he has one).

name: Steph Curry
blurb: Greatest shooter alive. From 30 feet or from a mail-in envelope.

name: Billie Eilish
blurb: Whispers her songs. Her ballot counts at full volume anyway.

name: Kevin Hart
blurb: Small comedian, regular-size ballot.

name: Leonardo DiCaprio
blurb: His vote, unlike his girlfriends, is over 25.

name: Ye
blurb: Once ran for president. Still just the one vote, like everybody else.

name: Gavin Newsom
blurb: The hair alone feels like it should count as a second vote. It does not.

name: Peyton Manning
blurb: OMAHA! OMAHA! is not a valid write-in candidate.

name: John Elway
blurb: The Drive was 98 yards. His polling place is probably closer.

name: Lauren Boebert
blurb: Very loud. Ballot is the same size as everyone else's.

name: Michael Bolton
blurb: How can we be voters if we can't be friends?

name: Bobby Valentine
blurb: Once snuck back into a dugout in a disguise. Can't sneak a second ballot though.

name: Joe Biden
blurb: Amtrak's most loyal customer. Delaware's most famous voter.

name: Jill Biden
blurb: Doctor, professor, and holder of exactly one Delaware ballot.

name: Muriel Bowser
blurb: Runs the capital. Gets no senators. Cancel respectfully.

name: A Random Lobbyist
blurb: Influences thousands of votes professionally. Casts exactly one personally.

name: Donald Trump
blurb: Whatever else is true, in Palm Beach he is one (1) voter.

name: Jeff Bezos
blurb: Two-day shipping on everything except election results.

name: Tom Brady
blurb: Seven rings. One vote. No overtime rules in elections.

name: Dave Portnoy
blurb: One bite, everybody knows the rules: one ballot each.

name: Pitbull
blurb: Mr. Worldwide is, electorally speaking, Mr. One Precinct in Miami-Dade.

name: Serena Williams
blurb: 23 Grand Slams. The ballot box does not have a challenge system.

name: Ken Griffin
blurb: Moves billions. On election day, moves one lever like the rest of us.

name: Tyler Perry
blurb: Owns a whole studio lot. The precinct still only hands him one ballot.

name: Usher
blurb: Yeah! (to voting once, in Georgia).

name: Ludacris
blurb: Rolls out (to his polling place, area code 404).

name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
blurb: Extremely canceled-about, statistically.

name: Killer Mike
blurb: Half of Run the Jewels. Owner of one whole Atlanta ballot.

name: Roseanne Barr
blurb: Grows macadamia nuts on the Big Island. Harvests one ballot per cycle.

name: Woody Harrelson
blurb: True Detective, one true ballot, somewhere on Maui.

name: Bethany Hamilton
blurb: Surfs giant waves. Votes in a normal-sized booth.

name: Aaron Paul
blurb: Yeah Mr. White! Yeah civic participation!

name: Demi Moore
blurb: A ghost of Hollywood glamour haunting a Sun Valley precinct.

name: Barack Obama
blurb: Yes he can (vote once, in Illinois, like anyone).

name: Michelle Obama
blurb: When they go low, she goes to her polling place.

name: Chance the Rapper
blurb: Independent as they come — the label AND the voter registration.

name: JB Pritzker
blurb: Billionaire governor. The ballot booth is the one place he's average.

name: John Mellencamp
blurb: Little pink houses, one little pink ballot.

name: Mike Pence
blurb: Won't dine alone with anyone but his ballot.

name: Caitlin Clark
blurb: Logo threes from way downtown. Votes from normal distance.

name: Chuck Grassley
blurb: Has tweeted about hitting a deer. Will definitely hit his polling place.

name: Kim Reynolds
blurb: Governor of Iowa, holder of one Iowa ballot.

name: Travis Kelce
blurb: You might know his girlfriend. She votes in Tennessee. He's the Kansas one.

name: Laura Kelly
blurb: Middle-of-the-road governor, middle-of-the-ballot voter.

name: Mitch McConnell
blurb: Has blocked many votes. Cannot block yours.

name: Rand Paul
blurb: Will filibuster anything except his own trip to the polls.

name: Jack Harlow
blurb: First Class rapper, standard-class ballot, Louisville precinct.

name: James Carville
blurb: It's the economy, and also it's one vote per person, stupid.

name: John Kennedy (Senator)
blurb: Not that John Kennedy. This one votes in Louisiana and talks like a folk tale.

name: Stephen King
blurb: Has written scarier things than your opposing ballot. Barely.

name: Susan Collins
blurb: Is reportedly concerned about this website.

name: Wes Moore
blurb: Governor, author, veteran, single-ballot-holder.

name: Kevin Plank
blurb: Under Armour founder. Sweat-wicking technology does not extend to elections.

name: Elizabeth Warren
blurb: Has a plan for that. 'That' being her one vote.

name: Aly Raisman
blurb: Gold-medal floor routine. Ballots are scored pass/fail.

name: Bill Burr
blurb: Angry about everything. His ballot, though: calm, single, counted.

name: Eminem
blurb: You only get one shot. And one ballot. Do not miss your chance.

name: Gretchen Whitmer
blurb: Big Gretch votes like everyone else: once.

name: Dan Gilbert
blurb: Owns half of downtown Detroit and exactly one one-hundredth of a hundred votes.

name: Tim Walz
blurb: Will vote, then probably fix your snowblower.

name: Ilhan Omar
blurb: Squad member. Ballot: solo.

name: Morgan Freeman
blurb: If his vote could narrate itself, it would still only count once.

name: Brett Favre
blurb: Famous for un-retiring. Votes, however, cannot be un-cast.

name: Patrick Mahomes
blurb: No-look passes, yes-look voting.

name: Josh Hawley
blurb: Runs fast. Votes once.

name: Glenn Close
blurb: Eight Oscar nominations, zero extra ballots. Fatal Attraction to democracy.

name: Huey Lewis
blurb: The power of love is nothing compared to the power of one Montana ballot.

name: Ryan Zinke
blurb: Rode a horse to work once. Presumably drives to the polls.

name: Warren Buffett
blurb: The Oracle of Omaha's vote yields the same return as yours: exactly one vote.

name: Larry the Cable Guy
blurb: Git-R-Done (voter registration, then an actual voting plan).

name: Mark Wahlberg
blurb: Wakes up at 3:30am. Polls don't open until 7. He waits.

name: Mike Tyson
blurb: Everyone has a plan until they punch their ballot. (Please just fill in the oval.)

name: Andre Agassi
blurb: Image is everything. Turnout is the only thing.

name: Wayne Newton
blurb: Danke Schoen for registering to vote.

name: Chris Sununu
blurb: Live free AND vote.

name: Jeanne Shaheen
blurb: First woman elected both governor and senator. Number of ballots: still one.

name: Bruce Springsteen
blurb: Born to run… to his polling place in the great state of New Jersey.

name: Jon Bon Jovi
blurb: It's his vote. It's now or never. He ain't gonna live forever.

name: Queen Latifah
blurb: Royalty in name; in the voting booth, a commoner like the rest of us.

name: George R.R. Martin
blurb: Will absolutely finish voting before finishing The Winds of Winter.

name: Michelle Lujan Grisham
blurb: Governor of the Land of Enchantment, home of enchantingly normal one-person-one-vote rules.

name: Jerry Seinfeld
blurb: What's the deal with everyone only getting one vote? (It's the law, Jerry.)

name: Robert De Niro
blurb: You talkin' to my ballot?

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
blurb: Represents the Bronx and Queens. Votes as exactly one of them.

name: Chuck Schumer
blurb: Senate leader. Brooklyn voter. Ranks amendments AND bagels.

name: Jimmy Fallon
blurb: Will laugh supportively at whatever his ballot says.

name: Scarlett Johansson
blurb: Black Widow's vote is subject to the same laws of arithmetic as yours.

name: Bill Belichick
blurb: On to Cincinnati. But first, a Chapel Hill polling place. No further questions.

name: Zach Galifianakis
blurb: Between two ferns grows one North Carolina ballot.

name: Josh Stein
blurb: Governor. Voter. Alphabetically convenient.

name: Josh Duhamel
blurb: North Dakota doesn't even require voter registration. He has no excuse. Neither do you (if you live there).

name: Doug Burgum
blurb: Software billionaire turned politician. Compiles one ballot per election.

name: Dave Chappelle
blurb: Yellow Springs' most famous resident. His vote is not a bit.

name: Joe Burrow
blurb: Joe Cool in the pocket and, presumably, in the voting booth.

name: Machine Gun Kelly
blurb: Cleveland's loudest. His ballot is filled out in normal pen, probably.

name: JD Vance
blurb: Vice President. Still votes absentee-or-otherwise as one Ohioan.

name: Blake Shelton
blurb: God gave him you, and gave him one vote in Oklahoma.

name: Kevin Stitt
blurb: Governor Stitt's ballot: also just one sheet of paper.

name: Phil Knight
blurb: Just Do It (vote — Oregon mails you the ballot, there is no excuse).

name: Damian Lillard
blurb: Dame Time is whenever ballots are due in Oregon.

name: M. Night Shyamalan
blurb: Twist ending: his vote counts exactly once, no surprises.

name: Jason Kelce
blurb: Retired Eagle, shirtless in the cold, fully clothed at his precinct.

name: John Fetterman
blurb: Votes in a hoodie. It's allowed. Look it up.

name: James Woods
blurb: Extremely online. Votes offline, in Rhode Island, once.

name: Dan McKee
blurb: You've never heard of him. He's the governor. He definitely votes. Shouldn't you?

name: Darius Rucker
blurb: Only wanna vote with you, in Charleston, one time per election.

name: Bill Murray
blurb: Might show up to your polling place, wash your dishes, and leave. Unconfirmed.

name: Lindsey Graham
blurb: Has strong feelings, subject to change. His vote count does not: one.

name: Kristi Noem
blurb: One vote. We checked on the dog's registration; dogs cannot vote.

name: John Thune
blurb: Senate Majority Leader. In Sioux Falls: guy in line at the polls.

name: Dolly Parton
blurb: Working 9 to 5, voting once per election. WHY would you cancel Dolly? Monster.

name: Taylor Swift
blurb: Reportedly votes in Tennessee. Her Rhode Island beach house cannot vote. Are you ready for it?

name: Kid Rock
blurb: Bawitdaba da ballot da bang, da bang.

name: Morgan Wallen
blurb: Last night we let democracy talk.

name: Justin Timberlake
blurb: Can't stop the feeling (of civic duty).

name: Reese Witherspoon
blurb: What, like it's hard? (Voting. It isn't. Do it.)

name: Elon Musk
blurb: Owns rockets, satellites, and a social network. At the polls: one (1) vote.

name: Joe Rogan
blurb: Have you tried canceling his vote though? Jamie, pull up the turnout stats.

name: Matthew McConaughey
blurb: Alright, alright, alright: register, plan, vote.

name: Simone Biles
blurb: Does things no human has done. Voting twice is not one of them.

name: Mark Cuban
blurb: For that offer, I'm out. For voting? He's in. Once.

name: Ted Cruz
blurb: Will be in-state on election day. Probably.

name: Chip & Joanna Gaines
blurb: They can renovate anything except the number of votes they get (two, total, combined).

name: Post Malone
blurb: Sunflower, rockstar, registered somewhere near Salt Lake, reportedly.

name: Mitt Romney
blurb: Binders full of ballots would be illegal. He has one, like you.

name: Bernie Sanders
blurb: He is once again asking you to make a voting plan.

name: Phil Scott
blurb: Republican governor of the Bernie state. His vote and Bernie's may already cancel out.

name: Glenn Youngkin
blurb: Very tall. Ballot: standard size.

name: Mark Warner
blurb: Senator, former governor, one-time holder of one vote at a time.

name: Bill Gates
blurb: His ballot has no blue screen. It's paper. Washington mails it to him.

name: Macklemore
blurb: Thrift Shop economics: your vote costs $0 and is worth exactly his.

name: Sue Bird
blurb: Greatest point guard ever. Assists don't count at the ballot box; turnout does.

name: Jim Justice
blurb: Senator, 6'7", travels with an English bulldog named Babydog. Babydog cannot vote. Jim can. Once.

name: Brad Paisley
blurb: West Virginia's favorite son. (Votes wherever he's registered, which is his business.)

name: Tony Evers
blurb: Holy mackerel, folks: one vote each.

name: Ron Johnson
blurb: A senator whose vote weighs the same as a cheese curd's owner's. Yours.

name: Charlie Berens
blurb: Keep 'er movin' — to the polls, ope, sorry, just gonna sneak right past ya to vote.

name: Harrison Ford
blurb: Indiana Jones, Han Solo, and one guy in line at a Jackson Hole precinct.

name: Liz Cheney
blurb: Wyoming has fewer people than many celebrities have staff. Her vote is proportionally enormous.
